name: Olive Oil Greenway and Cueva de los Murciélagos Loop
name_original: Zuheros, Luque, Baena, Doña Mencía – ruta circular
description: This difficult 25.1-mile mountain bike route through Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park offers challenging climbs and scenic Vía Verde del Acei
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Zuheros are set within the Sierra Subbética Natural Park, a UNESCO Geopark known for its dramatic limestone cliffs, extensive olive groves, and ancient oak forests. The region offers diverse terrain, from the gentle gradients of the Vía Verde del Aceite to more challenging climbs across craggy mountains. This landscape provides a variety of routes suitable for different skill levels, winding through picturesque countryside and past historical sites. Best mountain bike trails around Zuheros The most popular mountain bike trail is Olive Oil Greenway and Cueva de los Murciélagos Loop, a 25.1 miles (40.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route combines sections of the Vía Verde del Aceite with climbs towards the Cueva de los Murciélagos. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Steam locomotive - Mikado – San Marcos Bridge loop from Doña Mencía, an easy 18.0 miles (29.0 km) path. This route follows the Vía Verde del Aceite, passing through olive groves and over old railway viaducts. Local mountain bikers also love the La Sima Viaduct – San Marcos Bridge loop from Doña Mencía, a 65.3 miles (105.1 km) trail leading through the Sierra Subbética Natural Park, often completed in about 6 hours 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Zuheros is defined by the Vía Verde del Aceite, rugged limestone cliffs, and vast olive groves. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy greenway rides to demanding trails within the natural park. The routes in Zuheros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1,200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Zuheros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Zuheros?
A: There are over 130 mountain bike trails around Zuheros, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are set within the stunning Sierra Subbética Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Zuheros?
A: The terrain around Zuheros is incredibly diverse. You'll find everything from the relatively flat and easy gradients of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40387703" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt;, which follows a disused railway line through olive groves, to more challenging climbs and descents across the craggy mountains of the Sierra Subbética Natural Park. Expect limestone cliffs, ancient oak forests, and picturesque countryside.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy mountain bike trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40387703" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Steam locomotive - Mikado – San Marcos Bridge loop from Doña Mencía&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 29 km (18 miles) path that follows the Vía Verde del Aceite, perfect for a relaxed ride through olive groves and over old railway viaducts. This route is generally suitable for families looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986646459/olive-oil-greenway-and-cueva-de-los-murcielagos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olive Oil Greenway and Cueva de los Murciélagos Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 40.4 km (25.1 miles) trail that combines sections of the Vía Verde del Aceite with significant climbs towards the Cueva de los Murciélagos. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31411580" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaduct number 2 – Rest area km 1 loop from Doña Mencía&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 146.9 km (91.3 miles) route with substantial elevation changes.

Q: What landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer stunning views and pass by significant landmarks. You can explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zuheros Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which provides panoramic views of the village and surrounding landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2640138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Murciélagos&lt;/a&gt; (Cave of the Bats), one of Andalusia's most beautiful caves, is also accessible from some routes. The Vía Verde del Aceite features repurposed old railway stations, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/893899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doña Mencía Station&lt;/a&gt;, which often serve as cafes or visitor centers.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Zuheros?
A: Yes, many routes around Zuheros are designed as circular loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40386670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sima Viaduct – San Marcos Bridge loop from Doña Mencía&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 105.1 km (65.3 miles) circular trail that takes you through the heart of the Sierra Subbética Natural Park.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails can vary, the natural park environment generally welcomes well-behaved dogs on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage or park rules, especially in protected areas like the Cañón del Río Bailón, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Zuheros?
A: The best seasons for mountain biking in Zuheros are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these periods, the weather is milder and more comfortable for cycling, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winters are generally mild but can have occasional rain.

Q: Where can I park my car when mountain biking in Zuheros?
A: Zuheros and the towns along the Vía Verde del Aceite, such as Doña Mencía, offer various parking options. Look for designated parking areas near the start points of popular trails or in the villages themselves. Many of the old railway stations along the Vía Verde have parking facilities.

Q: Is bike rental available in Zuheros or nearby?
A: Yes, bike rental services are readily available in the region, particularly in towns along the Vía Verde del Aceite. This makes it convenient for visitors who prefer not to bring their own equipment to explore the trails around Zuheros.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking?
A: The Sierra Subbética Natural Park is rich in flora and fauna. While cycling, you might spot various bird species, including eagles and vultures, especially in the more rugged areas. Ibex are also known to inhabit the limestone cliffs. The Cueva de los Murciélagos is home to several bat species.

Q: Are there cafes or facilities along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, especially along the Vía Verde del Aceite. Many of the old railway stations have been repurposed into cafes, restaurants, or visitor centers, offering refreshments and facilities. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4751177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Restaurant and Olive Oil Interpretation Center in the Old Luque Station&lt;/a&gt; is a great stop. Zuheros itself also has cafes and restaurants where you can refuel.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Zuheros?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra Subbética Natural Park, the unique experience of cycling the Vía Verde del Aceite through endless olive groves, and the variety of trails catering to different skill levels.
tips: The viewpoint gives you impressive views of the canyon formed by the Bailón River and the town of Zuheros.
It was one of the bastions of the caliphate and today it is one of the most beautiful and ancient medieval towns in the province of Córdoba. The castle of Luque is the great attraction.
This bridge crosses the N-432 road to continue on our route through the Via Verde de la Subbética. The Luque station is a perfect place to recharge your batteries.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Zuheros, Luque, Baena, Doña Mencía circular route?
faqs.answer: This route primarily follows sections of the Vía Verde del Aceite, an old railway line. You'll experience well-maintained paths, but also challenging unpaved segments with deep cuts and rocky formations. Expect vast olive groves, impressive viaducts like the one over the Bailón River, and rehabilitated tunnels, such as the 140-meter-long Plantío tunnel (which is unlit but passable).

faqs.question: What level of fitness and skill is required for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult mountain bike ride, requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills. With significant elevation gains (around 939 meters), some sections may even require you to push your bike. It's not suitable for beginners.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or features will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning views, including the View over Zuheros and the Bailón Gorge, with the majestic Zuheros Castle perched on a rock. You'll also pass by Luque Castle and cross impressive structures like the Bridge on the Vía Verde de la Subbética at Luque Station, as well as other viaducts over the Guadajoz and Víboras rivers.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in the towns of Zuheros, Luque, Baena, or Doña Mencía?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, all four towns offer access points to the Vía Verde del Aceite. Doña Mencía is particularly bike-friendly, with a bike lane connecting its historic center directly to the Vía Verde, which might offer convenient parking options. Look for designated parking near the old railway stations, as many have been repurposed for trail users.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 40.4 km circular mountain bike route is approximately 3 hours and 26 minutes. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and the specific conditions on the day.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route integrates with several other trails. It extensively uses sections of the Vía Verde del Aceite and also intersects with parts of the Camino Mozárabe desde Málaga and the Vía Verde Guadajoz Subbética.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: To avoid extreme heat, the best times to ride this trail are generally spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are more moderate, making the challenging climbs and longer distances more enjoyable. Summers can be very hot in Andalusia.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on the Vía Verde del Aceite and similar greenways, but they should always be kept on a leash to ensure their safety and the comfort of other trail users, as well as to protect local wildlife. Please be mindful of local regulations within the towns and the natural park.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits required or entrance fees to access the Vía Verde del Aceite or the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park for recreational activities like mountain biking. However, always respect park regulations, especially concerning waste disposal and wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or find amenities along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the towns of Zuheros, Luque, Baena, and Doña Mencía offer various amenities. Many old railway stations along the Vía Verde del Aceite have been repurposed into welcoming rest stops, restaurants, or even accommodation, providing convenient places to refuel and take a break.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for riding this route?
faqs.answer: While much of the Vía Verde is well-maintained, the circular nature of this route means you'll encounter significant elevation changes and some unpaved, rocky sections that demand good bike handling skills. An insider tip is to carry sufficient water, especially during warmer months, as some stretches between towns can be exposed. Consider starting early to avoid midday heat and to fully enjoy the scenic views without rush.
